title: Scientists Find a Special Protein That Malaria Parasites Need to Live
summary: Scientists have discovered a special protein called ARK1 that malaria parasites cannot live without. Malaria is a serious disease caused by tiny parasites that spread through mosquito bites. These parasites grow and divide inside people and mosquitoes to make more parasites and spread the disease. The researchers found that ARK1 acts like a traffic controller, helping the parasites divide properly by organizing their genetic material. When scientists turned off ARK1 in lab tests, the parasites could not divide correctly and stopped growing. This means ARK1 is a key target for new medicines to fight malaria. The protein is different from similar ones in human cells, so drugs could target it without harming people. This discovery could lead to better treatments to stop malaria and save lives.

tiny living things that live inside other animals or people and can make them sick
when one cell splits into two to make more cells
a special protein that malaria parasites need to grow and divide

options: It acts like a traffic controller for dividing
It stops people from getting sick
questions.correct_answer: It acts like a traffic controller for dividing

options: The parasites cannot divide and stop growing
questions.correct_answer: The parasites cannot divide and stop growing

options: It is different from human proteins, so drugs won't harm people

questions.correct_answer: It is different from human proteins, so drugs won't harm people

questions.question: What disease is caused by the parasites mentioned in the article?

background_read: Malaria is a disease that affects many people around the world, especially in warm places. It is caused by tiny parasites that live in mosquitoes. When a mosquito bites a person, it can pass the parasites into their blood. Inside the person, the parasites grow and multiply, making them feel sick with fever and chills. Scientists study how these parasites work to find ways to stop them. Proteins are important parts of cells that help them do their jobs. By understanding proteins like ARK1, researchers hope to create new medicines that can kill the parasites without hurting people, which could help prevent malaria and save lives.
Article_Structure: WHO: Scientists from universities and institutes around the world
WHAT: Discovered a protein called ARK1 that malaria parasites need to divide and survive
WHERE: In laboratories studying malaria parasites
WHY: To find new targets for drugs to treat and prevent malaria

perspectives.description: Scientists found ARK1 is essential for malaria parasite growth, offering a new way to fight the disease.
